_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Infantile hypertrophic pyloric stenosis leading to gastric outlet obstruction in pediatric patients.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management
USG Abdomen/ Upper Gastrointestinal Endoscopy/ Barium meal series

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Are clinical notes with detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, and indication for procedure available?
Did clinical presentation and imaging confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge available?

common_queries: Clinical presentation and imaging are not indicative of surgery for Pyloromyotomy.
Mandatory imaging (USG Abdomen/ Upper Gastrointestinal Endoscopy/ Barium meal series) not submitted for pre-authorization.
Detailed operative notes are missing or do not adequately describe the surgical steps for Pyloromyotomy.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with daily vitals and treatment details are not submitted for claim processing.
Diagnosis of Infantile hypertrophic pyloric stenosis is not adequately supported by clinical features or imaging.

clinical_key_pointers: PEDIATRICS - Etiology: Infantile hypertrophic pyloric stenosis. In this condition there is hypertrophy involving the pyloric antral circular muscle fibres. Duodenum is normal. The lumen is so much narrowed as to give rise to pyloric obstruction.

PEDIATRICS - Clinical features: Incidence: 3-5/1000 births. The child is normal at birth and the symptoms appear around 6-8 weeks. First symptom is vomiting. It is projectile, forcible and does not contain bile. A visible gastric peristalsis (VGP) can be seen especially when the mother feeds the child. Loss of weight-dehydration; Constipation and oliguria are the features; Per abdomen-hypertrophied thickened pylorus can be felt as a mass in the right hypochondrium-'Olive like mass'.
PEDIATRICS - Treatment: Correction of dehydration and electrolyte disturbance; Laparotomy; Ramstedt's pyloromyotomy is the surgical treatment.
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: Correction of dehydration and electrolyte disturbance
